data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Apple's website is likely built on a microservices architecture, allowing for modular components to be developed and deployed independently.
data.technical_spec: Use of containerization technologies and RESTful APIs to independently deploy and manage services.

data.title: Content Delivery Network
data.description: A global content delivery network (CDN) is used to provide fast and reliable access to users worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Integration of CDN services like Akamai or Cloudflare for caching and load balancing.

data.title: Responsive Design
data.description: The site is designed to be fully responsive, ensuring it displays correctly on a wide range of devices and screen sizes.
data.technical_spec: Adaptive techniques with media queries in CSS, fluid layouts, and flexible grids.

data.title: Single Page Application
data.description: Dynamic user interactions without full page reloads, increasing speed and user engagement.
data.technical_spec: Implementation using frameworks like React or Angular across key website areas.

data.title: Server-Side Rendering
data.description: Utilizes server-side rendering to enhance SEO and ensure faster initial loading times.
data.technical_spec: Frameworks such as Next.js could be utilized for rendering pages on the server.
